title: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

content.text: Author’s noteMany future lessons on this site make use of CTAD. If you’re compiling these examples using the C++14 standard (or older), you’ll get an error about missing template arguments. You’ll need to explicitly add such arguments to the example to make it compile.

Many future lessons on this site make use of CTAD. If you’re compiling these examples using the C++14 standard (or older), you’ll get an error about missing template arguments. You’ll need to explicitly add such arguments to the example to make it compile.

TipC++20 added the ability for the compiler to automatically generate deduction guides for aggregates, so deduction guides should only need to be provided for C++17 compatibility.Because of this, the version ofPairwithout the deduction guides should compile in C++20.std::pair(and other standard library template types) come with pre-defined deduction guides, which is why our example above that usesstd::paircompiles fine in C++17 without us having to provide deduction guides ourselves.

C++20 added the ability for the compiler to automatically generate deduction guides for aggregates, so deduction guides should only need to be provided for C++17 compatibility.Because of this, the version ofPairwithout the deduction guides should compile in C++20.std::pair(and other standard library template types) come with pre-defined deduction guides, which is why our example above that usesstd::paircompiles fine in C++17 without us having to provide deduction guides ourselves.

Because of this, the version ofPairwithout the deduction guides should compile in C++20.std::pair(and other standard library template types) come with pre-defined deduction guides, which is why our example above that usesstd::paircompiles fine in C++17 without us having to provide deduction guides ourselves.

std::pair(and other standard library template types) come with pre-defined deduction guides, which is why our example above that usesstd::paircompiles fine in C++17 without us having to provide deduction guides ourselves.
For advanced readersNon-aggregates don’t need deduction guides in C++17 because the presence of a constructor serves the same purpose.
Non-aggregates don’t need deduction guides in C++17 because the presence of a constructor serves the same purpose.
